frames.name: Drug Pricing Policy Implementation
frames.description: Focuses on the Trump administration's 'most-favored nation' drug pricing policy as a concrete policy initiative gaining momentum, with CMS actively expanding Medicaid pilot programs
frames.emphasis: Policy mechanics, implementation details, and potential for higher international drug prices as a trade-off

frames.name: Political Strategy and Messaging
frames.description: Frames healthcare policy through the lens of political positioning, showing the Trump administration attempting to pivot away from controversial vaccine policies toward more popular issues like drug pricing
frames.emphasis: Political calculations, voter appeal, and the challenge of controlling the news cycle narrative

frames.name: Public Health Expert Criticism
frames.description: Presents vaccine policy through the perspective of established medical authorities, featuring criticism from vaccine pioneer Stanley Plotkin
frames.emphasis: Scientific credibility, moral dimensions of vaccine policy, and expert opposition to current approaches

outlierPerspectives: The characterization of anti-vaccine government positions as both 'stupid' and 'immoral' by a 93-year-old vaccine legend - most coverage would likely focus on either scientific or political aspects, not moral condemnation
The specific mention of potential higher drug prices abroad as a consequence of most-favored nation policies - this international impact angle appears unique
missingContext: Specific details about what the 'most-favored nation' policy actually entails and how it would work
Quantitative data on current vaccination rates and the scale of any decline
Details about which specific vaccine policies the Trump administration is implementing or considering
Information about the Medicaid pilot program's scope, timeline, and participating drugmakers
Context about the Henrietta Lacks settlement with Novartis and its significance
